title: Unable to push manifest list to quay.io
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying to create a (multi-arch) manifest with two images and push that to quay.io. The first attempt works without any issues, but if the manifest already exists on the destination, the process fails on quay.io while it does work on docker.io. It could be that the issue therefore lies with quay.io and I've to report it there too.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

There is an example on quay.io which you can use to reproduce. It's using the images `quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-aarch64` and `quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-amd64`
                                                                                                           
1. `podman pull quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-amd64 quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-aarch64`
2. `podman manifest create quay.io/<user>/<repo>:<tag>`
3. `podman manifest add "quay.io/<user>/<repo>:<tag>"  quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-amd64 quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master-arch64` 
4. `podman manifest push --all "quay.io/<user>/<repo>:<tag>" "docker://quay.io/<user>/<repo>:<tag>"`

**Describe the results you received:**

After the second push attempt (probably with updated images so there is actually a change to push) the command fails:

```
Getting image list signatures
Copying 2 of 2 images in list
[... lot of copying logs ... ]
Storing signatures
Writing manifest list to image destination
Error: error pushing manifest quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master to docker://quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container:master: Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error uploading manifest master to quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container: manifest invalid: manifest invalid), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest master to quay.io/othermo/kapacitor-container: manifest invalid: manifest invalid)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I'd expect the manifest push to work.

When using the same command against docker.io it works without a problem.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This might be a problem in quay.io, so I'll also investigate in that direction.

The two images used in my case are build using different OSes and versions of podman as well as using different manifest versions. Podman manifest inspect outputs this:

```
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws",
            "size": 11162,
            "digest": "sha256:f3d3e5028d3157aa4541129753e53b92641918f8c1b28423e2a12fd2403856d2",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "arm64",
                "os": "linux"
            }
        },
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
            "size": 2014,
            "digest": "sha256:98b32a946c18122edb8fef2a3983e1ba60932d796e784a968c1aa0dbb97b7f27",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "amd64",
                "os": "linux"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 18:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This issues has similarities in appearance but differs from #8242 in that the initial push (using --all) works, but subsequent pushes fail. Also only the podman command was tried, working against docker.io but not quay.io.

comments.body:
@QiWang19 PTAL

@rhatdan This seems like a good candidate for the error clarification work we were talking about, that error is the stuff of nightmares.

Opened up a PR to cleanup the error a wee bit.

https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8370

@w4tsn For updating the image in the manifest list and pushing it. Will the workaround in this [explanation](https://lists.podman.io/archives/list/podman@lists.podman.io/message/EIYJSUW4FM4QXSESJK4GAUA74GOHLCUF/) work for you?

@QiWang19 apparently yes, since I'm the one who asked the question initially :D

Just to have it here for reference what I had to do to get it working:

- add `--format docker` to my build commands
- add `--format v2s2` to my push commands
- (maybe) update buildah from a version from july to latest

quay.io does not support OCI manifest type, only docker. That's way I had to ensure that my types are set to docker.

Besides the update of the error reporting of podman I don't know if podman could do a lot about this issue so I suppose it can be closed.
